status_notes: All 6 documents are genuinely separate PDFs (confirmed via research 2026-07-16) issued by different MLIT bureaus, not chapters of one umbrella document — 'logistics' in particular splits into 3 independent sub-sector guidelines (truck/motor freight, warehousing, maritime shipping), not one. All PDFs fetched successfully (not blocked) but are graphics/scan-heavy and resisted automated text extraction; a proper PDF/OCR tool would be needed for full parsing. No English versions were found for any of the 6.
official_name: Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism, Japan
notes: All 6 guidelines share a common title pattern ('Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the [sector]') and operate under Japan's Cybersecurity Policy for Critical Infrastructure Protection (secid:control/cyber.go.jp/cip-policy). Master index: https://www.mlit.go.jp/sogoseisaku/jouhouka/sosei_jouhouka9999.html (also lists a Water Supply and a Ports guideline not yet added here).

match_nodes.description: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Aviation Sector (air transport operators)

data.official_name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Aviation Sector
data.notes: Published by MLIT's Civil Aviation Bureau. Currently 6th-7th edition (revised April 2024/2026 depending on snapshot). Organized around organizational governance / risk management / crisis management / control-item categories. Covers cybersecurity requirements for airline operational systems (flight ops, reservation systems) as designated critical infrastructure. Japanese-only.

urls.note: Fetched successfully (200 OK); image/scan-heavy, text extraction failed in this pass.

match_nodes.description: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Airport Sector

data.official_name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Airport Sector
data.notes: Published by MLIT's Civil Aviation Bureau (same bureau as aviation-security, a separate document). Currently 4th edition (as of the April 2024/2026 revision cycle). Covers airport-operator systems (baggage, ground ops, facility management) distinct from airline-operator systems. Japanese-only.

urls.note: Not individually fetch-tested; hosted identically to the aviation PDF.

match_nodes.description: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Railway Sector

data.official_name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Railway Sector
data.notes: Published by MLIT's Railway Bureau. Currently 6th edition. Scope explicitly names train operation management, power management, and seat-reservation systems as protection targets. Japanese-only. Note: PDF URLs change between editions (link rot observed) — use the landing page to find the current file.

urls.note: Landing page (use this for the current link).
6th edition. Fetched successfully (200 OK); image/scan-heavy, text extraction failed in this pass.

match_nodes.description: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Logistics Sector (Truck/Motor Freight Transport)

data.official_name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Logistics Sector (Truck/Motor Freight Transport)
data.notes: Published by MLIT's Road Bureau/Automobile Bureau. Currently 2nd edition. One of 3 independent logistics sub-sector guidelines (see also logistics-warehousing-security and logistics-maritime-security) — 'logistics' is not a single document. Japanese-only.

urls.note: Landing page (lists truck-transport and warehousing guidelines together).
Fetched successfully (200 OK); image/scan-heavy, text extraction failed in this pass.

match_nodes.description: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Logistics Sector (Warehousing)

data.official_name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Logistics Sector (Warehousing)
data.notes: Published by MLIT's Road Bureau/Automobile Bureau. Currently 2nd edition. One of 3 independent logistics sub-sector guidelines. Japanese-only.

urls.note: Not individually fetch-tested; hosted the same way as the truck-freight PDF.

match_nodes.description: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Logistics Sector (Maritime Shipping Operations)

data.official_name: Safety Guideline for Ensuring Information Security in the Logistics Sector (Maritime Shipping Operations)
data.notes: Published by MLIT's Maritime Bureau (distinct from the Road/Automobile Bureau, which publishes the other 2 logistics sub-guidelines). 1st edition, established April 2024. Japanese-only.

urls.note: Not individually fetch-tested in this pass.
